cards.front: What is the RGB color model?
cards.back: A three-dimensional color model, commonly used in digital displays, where red, green, and blue are combined to produce a wide range of colors.

cards.front: What is the difference between additive and subtractive color models?
cards.back: Additive color models (RGB) combine light to produce colors, while subtractive color models (CMY) combine ink to absorb certain wavelengths of light and produce colors.
A technique used to display color images on devices that do not natively support color, by assigning a limited set of colors to the image and mapping them to the device's available colors.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the CMYK color model?
cards.back: To produce a wide range of colors in printing, by combining cyan, magenta, and yellow inks to absorb certain wavelengths of light and produce colors.
A technique used to display high-quality, full-color images on devices that support color, using the RGB color model and a high number of available colors.
